extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan outsmarted GAVIRIA Fernando by following the Quickstep train then launching from mid-pack with 200m remaining.
tactical_patterns: GAVIRIA positioned mid-peloton rather than front, suggesting confidence in late acceleration—but misjudged distance and got beaten.
Dimension Data led out excessively early for CAVENDISH, burning team resources 5.5km out while Lotto held positioning discipline.
Quickstep controlled final chicane at 1km despite sprinting for rival, revealing GAVIRIA's tactical parasitism on former team's infrastructure.
KRISTOFF in European champion jersey positioned perfectly at flamme rouge but lacked explosive acceleration, indicating one-day power doesn't translate to Tour sprints.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: read Quickstep's lead-out despite them working for GAVIRIA, then launched from farther back than rivals expected
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: positioned mid-pack expecting to use pure speed but misjudged timing, suggesting overconfidence after early stage wins
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: locked wheel-to-wheel with GAVIRIA at 1km but unable to come around, indicating sprint form adequate but not dominant
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: recovered to tenth after team led out 5.5km from finish, showing improved sprint form despite tactical waste
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: not in top 30 positioning, commentary noted possible confidence loss after switching from Quickstep's perfect lead-out infrastructure
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: European champion jersey visible throughout finale, perfect position at 1km but lacked finishing kick for podium
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: neutral: fourth place after being shouldered by Quickstep rider in final 500m, suggesting strong position compromised tactically
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: Cofidis worked for him through chicanes but only managed fifth, indicating team effort without individual firepower
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: positive: sixth place marked as unexpected by commentary, showing resurgent form without dedicated lead-out
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: commentary pre-identified him with only HAUSSLER for support, suggesting Bahrain lacking sprint infrastructure despite his form
rider_signals.RENSHAW Mark: neutral: visible searching for position mid-stage but Dimension Data's early pace-making wasted his lead-out value
rider_signals.LE GAC Olivier: negative: crashed before finale when peloton went wide on narrow approach, indicating positioning error under pressure
